summary: Name-squat placeholder for 'worker-build' with no payload in this version
details: The package ships only README.md and package.json — there is no main entrypoint, no lifecycle scripts (preinstall/install/postinstall/prepare), and no executable code. The README self-describes the package as a proof-of-concept occupying the unclaimed npm name 'worker-build', which mimics Cloudflare Workers / Service Workers build tooling. Installing this version produces no runtime behavior on the installer's machine and no network or filesystem activity. However, the name is being held under a pretextual identity: any future version published under 'worker-build' would be delivered to consumers who selected the name expecting Cloudflare-related tooling. Routing to human review so a maintainer can assess the impersonation risk and decide whether to request namespace remediation; this version itself is not an active supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
